summary: Sprint #9 (Observability & Backups, BLOCKER #9) — 19/19 backend tests PASS. Verified: shallow/deep/launch-check health endpoints, X-Request-ID generation+echo+propagation, JSON-structured logs (timestamp/level/logger/message/correlation_id/tenant_id/user_id/endpoint), PII sweep on auth login (auth_login_failed event uses extra={reason, user_id}, no email f-string leak; PII regex filter additionally scrubs at formatter level), Sentry skipped cleanly when SENTRY_DSN empty, Stripe webhook bad-signature still rejected, regression-clean on /api/auth/login (admin+member), /api/platform/stats, /api/portal/me, /api/portal/giving/history, /api/stripe/create-payment-intent (no 5xx), and backup.sh runs end-to-end with mongodump → produces gz artifacts in /tmp/solomon-bak.
critical_code_review_comments: core/observability.py:218-243 CorrelationIdMiddleware — generates 16-char hex id, propagates via contextvars, echoes back X-Request-ID. Also emits a request_completed log with duration_ms/method/path. Correct, idempotent.

core/observability.py:68-101 _PiiRedactionFilter — fail-soft regex scrub for email/PAN/SSN/sk_live_*. Filter applied at handler level so even if a route accidentally drops an email into a log message, it's masked before write. Defense-in-depth pairing with the f-string sweep is good.

core/observability.py:163-195 init_sentry — short-circuits when SENTRY_DSN empty (returns False). Verified via /api/health?deep=true → checks.sentry.status='disabled'. send_default_pii=False is correct given we already strip PII.

server.py:47-84 health endpoints — registered BEFORE middleware/auth so they always probe-200 even if downstream wiring is broken. Deep probe uses asyncio.wait_for(0.25s) for mongo ping → fail-fast. Returns 503 with degraded payload on mongo failure. Solid.

routes/auth.py:351,356,373 — login failures now use logging.warning('auth_login_failed', extra={'reason': ..., 'user_id': ...}). Verified in /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log: log lines contain reason='user_not_found' / 'password_mismatch' / 'no_password_hash' WITHOUT the input email. PII sweep landed correctly.

scripts/backup.sh — runs cleanly with mongodump, retains by --retain-days, supports --upload s3 hook. Smoke-tested with --target /tmp/solomon-bak --retain-days 7 → gz artifacts produced. Production-ready with the documented Atlas migration note.

Note (non-blocking): pythonjsonlogger import path uses the new `pythonjsonlogger.json` module (correct for the latest python-json-logger ≥3.x). Just flag if the requirements.txt pin is loose — make sure ≥3.0 is enforced so the import doesn't break on a minor downgrade.
test_credentials: admin@solomonai.us / Demo2026! (platform_admin) and member@abundant.church / Demo2026! (member) — both verified.
seed_data_creation: Tests issue ~3 invalid logins with synthetic TEST_*@solomon-pii-probe.test addresses (no DB write, just 401 paths). One anonymous PI create with TEST_pi_iter109_*@example.com (no Stripe charge — endpoint returns <500 either way). backup.sh writes gz dumps to /tmp/solomon-bak (ephemeral container path). No production data touched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sprint #9 BLOCKER #9 fully verified. Health endpoints, correlation IDs, JSON logs, PII redaction, Sentry-empty-DSN fallback, webhook signature gate, and mongodump backup are all green. Reusable regression file: /app/backend/tests/test_sprint9_observability_iter109.py — run after any future log/middleware change to catch PII regressions. To verify PII filter on routes that haven't been swept yet, add a test that hits the route with a TEST_*@solomon-pii-probe.test email and grep /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log for the verbatim string. The deep-health endpoint is now the canonical readiness probe — point any external monitor (e.g., UptimeRobot, Pingdom) at /api/health?deep=true; treat 503 as page-on-call.
rca of the issue: N/A — no issues found this iteration.
